preamble: The backing and rounding of a high front vowel /i/ to a back rounded vowel /o/, a process of vowel retraction often influenced by surrounding labial or velar consonants.

examples.note: Backing of /ɪ/ before a dark /l/ in some dialects, approaching /o/ or /u/.

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
